text: Here I am among the carefully crafted lines and boundaries of modern architecture, looking through glass into a vibrant intellectual display. My own reflection is faintly visible, mixed with interpretive shapes and colors. I feel curious yet slightly unsettled; the glass both separates and connects me to the ideas inside. The exhibit—bold, colorful, and explicit in its advocacy for research and development—sparks thoughts about human longing to innovate and belong. I sense a tension between observation and participation, between the cold order of the hallway and the electric excitement of knowledge just ahead. The space invites both inquiry and contemplation, and in this transitional moment, I am suspended between outside and in, visitor and participant.
